purpose: Call Tracker browser contract for live Vercel Supabase reads. Supabase owns the reporting views and count booleans; Vercel reads those low-maintenance API views at request time so call-tracker data can change without redeploying the static app. Reporting counts come from explicit countsAsDial, countsAsContact, countsAsMeetingSet, countsAsPostMeetingOutcome, and counts_as_* booleans, never from activity_kind alone.

eventFeed.plainEnglish: The Vercel API reads canonical call_log rows directly. Rows must carry proof fields plus count booleans so the browser never recomputes business meaning.

summaryHelper.plainEnglish: The Vercel API derives summary data from canonical call_log rows. Dials mirror the event-feed booleans. All-time visible contacts may include an explicit historical UI adjustment while Supabase facts remain strict.

deprecatedAliases.total_events: Do not display as Dials. Use dials.
deprecatedAliases.spoke_with: Do not display as Contacts. Use contacts.

sourceFamilies.rawMeaning: Outbound call work and call dispositions from task activity.
Pipeline lifecycle transitions. Meeting Set and task activity facts live here so proof sticks at ingest instead of being repaired later.
Post-meeting outcomes such as closed won, closed lost, no show, canceled, and reschedule pending.

ownershipContract.plainEnglish: A row can only be trusted for the dashboard when it carries active-operator materialization proof. Vercel should display these fields as proof, not recompute ownership.

cardBindings.plainEnglish: How many outbound attempts happened today.
How many meaningful contact/disposition outcomes happened today.
How many meetings were set today.

cardBindings.countRule: display data.ui.summaryCards.dials derived from call_log counts_as_dial
cardBindings.forbiddenRule: Do not display raw call_log row count as Dials.

cardBindings.countRule: display data.ui.summaryCards.contacts, including any explicit historical all-time contacts adjustment
cardBindings.forbiddenRule: Do not count post-meeting outcomes as Contacts.

domainOutcomeRules.plainEnglish: Call attempt 1 is an outbound dial but not a contact.
Call attempt 2 is an outbound dial but not a contact.
Call attempt 3 is an outbound dial but not a contact.
Unable to leave voicemail is dial only.
Not interested is a real contact outcome, not close lost.
Reached athlete, not parent, counts as dial and contact.
Too young is a contact outcome, not close lost.
Need to follow up is dial and contact.
Meeting Set counts as dial, contact, and meeting set.

liveSupabaseApi.plainEnglish: The workflow cron writes pipeline facts into Supabase only. Vercel serves the static app and the lightweight API route reads canonical call_log live.

Athlete is 6’5”. His mom’s side of the family is tall, with some people reaching 6’11”. He’s interested but admitted his finances are tight. He has NCSA experience and is recovering financially.

Serious about playing at the next level and Dad trains him hard.

Dad is a JV Basketball coach and weekends are best. Wants to discuss with his wife

manualCorrections.reason: Historical manual correction for contacts lost during mid-tracking implementation. Supabase facts remain strict; this adjusts all-time Vercel UI contacts/set-rate only.
